qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Unsanitized path parameter in filesystem plugin allows arbitrary file read and write
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Different vulnerable code and root cause: this finding is the filesystem plugin's unsanitized path-to-file-operation flow, not the auth_users custom package installation path or missing superuser gate described by the advisory.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Terminal plugin script endpoint passes user-controlled JSON field directly to bash -c enabling authenticated RCE
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Different vulnerable code and root cause: this finding is direct shell execution through the terminal plugin, not custom package installation through the auth_users authentication model by a non-superuser.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No final qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described vulnerable code/root cause. The advisory concerns an authorization bypass where an authenticated auth_users user can install a custom package despite not being superuser. The qualifying findings instead cover filesystem path traversal and terminal command execution.
evidence: Advisory GHSA-73jv-44c3-j5p2 describes custom package installation by a non-superuser authenticated through the auth_users plugin.
The filesystem_path_traversal finding reports plugins/filesystem/views.py path parameters flowing to file operations without confinement.
The terminal_rce finding reports plugins/terminal/views.py passing a JSON script field to bash -c.
Neither qualifying final finding reports the custom package installation task, the auth_users-specific non-superuser condition, or an equivalent missing superuser check on that source-to-sink path.
